bird.name: Tamaulipas pygmy owl
bird.summary: The Tamaulipas pygmy owl (Glaucidium sanchezi) is a species of owl in the family Strigidae. It is endemic to Mexico.

locations.name: 聖路易斯波托西州

relations.subject: Tamaulipas pygmy owl
relations.evidence: The Tamaulipas pygmy owl is endemic to Mexico where it is only found in the mountains of northeastern Mexico, in the northern part of the state of Hidalgo and the southeastern part of the state of San Luis Potosí. Its habitat is moist evergreen forest, montane forest and cloud forest at altitudes between about

relations.subject: Tamaulipas pygmy owl
relations.evidence: However, at , it is slightly heavier than the long-whiskered owlet and the elf owl. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests
